channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: TMC - Defects -other CW6 problem, 9th April
item.description:
Depression CW Noticeable depression on junction. Unable to fully measure, need longer level 7000x5000x40mm approx Road closure Stats (CW6 Rutting&Depressions)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 9th April
item.description:
Please can an enquiry be raised for the GCC Tree Officer to undertake an inspection of the tree outside 37 Moselle Drive, Churchdown given the concerns raised below by County Cllr Stewart Dove ? I have had a concern from a resident about a cherry tree that has been damaged in a storm, with a branch that fell off; and there is a worry that more problems are likely. The resident feels that it should be pollarded... The tree is outside 37 Moselle Drive, GL3 2RX, and is on public land (ie not within someone's garden) Apparently the resident reported on 'fix my street', and 'the council removed the fallen branch' and reported that the issue was dealt with; however neighbours feel the tree is not safe in its current condition
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Children Safety Concern, no pavement to bus, 9th April
item.description:
Wr alteady live at new Harper Crew development, there is currently no pavement leading to the bus stop (unmarked) and no safe walking route. Children are forced to walk on or very close to the road on the grass and mud, which creates a significant safety risk, especially during busy traffic hours. After school children jump on the grass, no safety crossing the road... speed sign change from 40 to 30 just before the bent and not many cars slow down at this point, and from other side start to speed up... The road is busy, and without a pavement or temporary walking path, this situation is dangerous and unacceptable.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stoke Road, Stoke Orchard, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 9th April
item.description:
The roads here are super bumpy and are in incredibly bad condition - Damaged tires
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In the road on approach to Willow Farm, 9th April
item.description:
Several large potholes near this location. Vehicles have to swerve around them to avoid damage to vehicles tyres/wheels. Men from highways have marked some potholes with white paint further down into the village but did not inspect the road near to Willow Farm
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Badgeworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Signs & Street Furniture problem, 9th April
item.description:
Damaged KL Bollard CW ENWD Stop and go TM MAKE SAFE WITH TEMPORARY KL SIGN Enquiry raised for Street Lighting (Damaged Bollard)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard shell is damaged/vandalised problem, 9th April
item.description:
Damaged Illuminated KL Bollard. Asset Number 113300. Please pass on to Street Lighting to arrange reinstatement. Making safe with temporary sign.
Report on FixMyStreet
